_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
condition: Biopsy- Cervical, Endometrial EA/ ECC; Vulvar; Polypectomy
procedure_name: Biopsy- Cervical, Endometrial EA/ ECC; Vulvar; Polypectomy
source_files: Biopsy- Cervical, Endometrial EA ECC; Vulvar; Polypectomy_Medco.pdf
Biopsy- Cervical, Endometrial EA ECC; Vulvar; Polypectomy_PPD.CPD.pdf
min_doctor_qualification: MBBS with 6 months training in OBGYN
clinical_indications: Abnormality found during a PAP test/ VIA/VILI (Visual examination of the uterine cervix, after application of 5% acetic acid (VIA) and/or of Lugol's iodine (VILI))/Colposcopy
Detection of precancerous hyperplasia and atypia
Follow-up of previously diagnosed endometrial hyperplasia
Abnormal Papanicolaou smear with atypical cells favoring endometrial origin
Any patient who reports or is found to have a any grossly suspicious vulvar lesion such as a confluent, wartlike mass must be thoroughly evaluated to rule out malignancy.
Biopsy must be performed on any suspicious lesions of the vulva, asymptomatic or symptomatic.
Change in the colour, elevation, or surface of a lesion
Small polyp (<3cm size) with easily accessible thin pedicle

preauth.label: Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure and advice for admission
Other investigation report (such as PAP test, HPV test, etc.)

claim.label: Detailed indoor case papers clearly indicating the need for performing the procedure
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination

ppd_preauth.q: Are detailed Clinical notes all vitals, detailed history, symptoms, signs, physical examination including local examination, indication for procedure and advice for admission available?
Was the clinical presentation, physical examination ± supporting investigations indicative of procedure?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed Indoor Case Papers available?
Are detailed procedure notes available with indications for the procedure?
Is discharge summary available with follow-up advice at the time of discharge?
Was Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen removed for histopathological examination submitted
Was there a correlation of biopsy lesion with histopathology report?

common_queries: Clinical presentation, physical examination, or supporting investigations are not indicative of the procedure.
Detailed Clinical notes with history, symptoms, signs, examination findings, indication for procedure, or advice for admission are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
Other investigation report (such as PAP test, HPV test) is missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed indoor case papers are missing or do not clearly indicate the need for performing the procedure at claim submission.
Detailed Procedure/Operative notes are missing at claim submission.
Histopathology form confirming submission of specimen is missing or does not confirm submission at claim submission.
Discharge summary with follow-up advice is missing at claim submission.
Biopsy lesion and histopathology report show no correlation.
Polyp size exceeds 3cm or pedicle is not easily accessible for polypectomy.
Procedure performed on a pregnant patient without strong suspicion of invasive cancer.
Procedure performed with active cervico-vaginal infection.
Procedure performed on a patient receiving anticoagulant therapy.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Secondary/ Tertiary care facility with a minor OT/ procedure room.
clinical_key_pointers: Biopsy- Cervical (Punch biopsy), Endometrial EA/ ECC; Vulvar; Polypectomy

Cervical biopsy can be done in several ways (Punch biopsy/ Cone biopsy/ Endocervical curettage (ECC)). This STG is applicable for Punch biopsy and endocervical curettage. For Cone Biopsy may refer to specific STG and corresponding package should be booked.
The tissue thus removed is sent for histopathology examination to test for abnormal/ precancerous/ cancerous conditions of cervix/ endometrium/vulva.
contraindications: Pregnancy (relative contraindication), should only be performed in cases of strong suspicion of invasive cancer
